+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a280
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Spatial rects go stale on scroll — fix click-to-focus and up/down nav reliability inside scrollable zones
+---
+## What
+
+Inside a scrollable container (notably a column with enough tasks to scroll), spatial focus is unreliable:
+
+- Clicking many cards does not focus them.
+- `nav.up` / `nav.down` from a focused card frequently produces no focus change (focus is lost).
+
+The root cause is a coordinate-system bug: **`<FocusZone>` and `<FocusScope>` push their rect to the kernel on mount and on `ResizeObserver` events only — never on scroll.** `getBoundingClientRect()` returns viewport-relative coordinates, and the user's scroll inside an ancestor container shifts every descendant's viewport-y while the kernel's stored rects stay fixed at their mount-time values. Beam-search and other rect-driven kernel operations then run on stale geometry and pick wrong candidates (or no candidate). Off-screen virtualised rows already get correct rects because the placeholder-registration hook in `column-view.tsx:367` re-runs on `scrollOffset` change — but **real-mounted primitives have no equivalent path**, so their rects diverge from the placeholder rects (which are in current-viewport coords) and from the user's actual on-screen layout.
+
+## Where the bug lives
+
+### Real-mounted primitives never refresh their rect on ancestor scroll
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx:422` and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:434` (the `SpatialFocusZoneBody` / `SpatialFocusScopeBody` register-effect) attach a `ResizeObserver` to the host node and call `updateRect` only when that observer fires. `ResizeObserver` watches box size; **scrolling an ancestor does not resize the host**, so the observer never fires on scroll, and the kernel's stored `(x, y)` for the host stays at its mount-time viewport position.
+
+The kernel uses these rects directly:
+
+- `BeamNavStrategy::next` (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`) scores candidates against the focused entry's rect and the in-beam tier preference. With one rect stale and another fresh (e.g. an off-screen placeholder re-pushed on scroll vs a real card whose mount-time rect is now invalid), the relative geometry is corrupted and "down" picks something the user cannot see.
+- `drill_in`'s cold-start fallback (`SpatialRegistry::drill_in`) sorts by rect top/left to pick the first child — stale rects produce nondeterministic "first child" picks across scroll positions.
+
+### Click-to-focus reliability — investigate alongside
+
+The click handler in `<FocusScope>` (`focus-scope.tsx:511`) calls `focus(key)` which dispatches `spatial_focus(key)` and is independent of rects, so on paper clicks should still focus. In practice the user reports clicks frequently fail inside scrolled columns. The likely contributors:
+
+1. **Race between virtualizer mount and click**. When a card scrolls into view, the column-view's placeholder hook unregisters the placeholder and the real `<EntityCard>` mounts, freshly minting its own `SpatialKey` (every `<FocusScope>` mints `crypto.randomUUID()` per mount — see `focus-scope.tsx:374`). If the user clicks before the real-card register IPC has completed, `focus(key)` arrives at the kernel for an unknown key and the kernel rejects/logs it.
+2. **Focus event lost after re-key**. The kernel's `focused_key` may still point at a previously-unregistered key (the placeholder that was unregistered when the card scrolled in, or a prior real-mount key from before a re-render). The new mount's `useFocusClaim(newKey, setFocused)` subscription has no event to wake on.
+3. **Stale rect breaks the focus-claim subscription path.** Less likely — subscription is by `SpatialKey`, not by rect. Pin via test before assuming.
+
+The investigation must determine whether (a) clicking reaches `focus(key)` with a key the kernel knows about and the kernel's `focus-changed` event reaches the React side carrying that key; and (b) whether the visible `<FocusIndicator>` renders, given that `useFocusClaim` is per-key. Tests below assert each step.
+
+## Approach
+
+### Fix the rect-staleness — primitive-level scroll listener
+
+In `<FocusZone>` and `<FocusScope>`'s spatial body, when the host mounts:
+
+1. Walk parent chain to find every scrollable ancestor (an element whose computed `overflow-y` or `overflow-x` is `auto` / `scroll`). Stop at `document.documentElement`.
+2. Attach a `passive` `scroll` listener on each. The listener calls `updateRect(key, rect)` with the current `getBoundingClientRect()`, throttled to one call per `requestAnimationFrame` (so a fast scroll produces O(frames) IPC calls, not O(events)).
+3. On unmount, detach all listeners.
+
+This sits at the same level as the existing `ResizeObserver`. Both writes coalesce in the kernel: `apply_batch` is idempotent on `SpatialKey`, and `updateRect` is a single-key overwrite.
+
+If the cost of "every primitive carries N scroll listeners up its parent chain" is a concern, a follow-up can centralise via a `ScrollObserver` context that tells descendants to re-measure — but ship the simple per-primitive listener first to fix the bug, and only revisit if profiling shows real-world cost.
+
+### Click-to-focus reliability — driven by tests
+
+Write the click-after-scroll tests first. If they fail because the kernel doesn't know the clicked key, the fix is one of:
+
+- Have the click handler `await registerEffect()` before calling `focus(key)`. (The register call is already async; the click handler can wait on the same promise the register effect produced.)
+- Block the click handler from running until the register has acknowledged.
+- Re-emit `focus-changed` for any newly-registered key whose moniker matches the current `focused_moniker` in the kernel — i.e. the kernel notices a key/moniker swap and re-points its focus claim.
+
+The tests below pin the behaviour. The implementer chooses the seam the failing test points at.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+All asserted by automated tests below — no manual smoke step.
+
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, the kernel's stored `(x, y, w, h)` for every real-mounted card matches its current on-screen `getBoundingClientRect()` (within 1 px tolerance for sub-pixel rounding). Asserted via a test that scrolls, then queries the registry's debug view (or via `spatial_navigate` outcomes that depend on accurate rects).
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.down` from the topmost visible card produces a focus change to a card the user can see (its rect is currently inside the column's viewport, OR is a placeholder that triggers an immediate `scrollIntoView` to bring the real card on-screen). The test asserts the new focused moniker is the next card in the column's content order.
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.up` from the bottommost visible card produces a focus change to the previous card in content order.
+- [ ] Clicking any visible card in a scrolled column focuses it — `useFocusedScope()` reports the card's moniker and `<FocusIndicator>` renders for that card. Asserted across at least three scroll positions (top, middle, near-bottom) so a one-off mount-race coincidence cannot pass.
+- [ ] Clicking a card immediately after it scrolls into view (no idle period between scroll-end and click) focuses it. Regression guard for the mount-vs-click race.
+- [ ] No regression: clicking and arrow-keying inside a non-scrollable zone (a column with few enough tasks that no scroll is active) keeps working as today.
+- [ ] No regression: cross-column nav (Right / Left across columns) still works correctly when the source column is mid-scroll.
+
+## Tests
+
+All tests are automated. No manual verification.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Mounts `<BoardView>` against the per-test backend the existing browser tests use. Builds a column with enough tasks (≥ 30) to force virtualization, drives the scroll container directly, and asserts on observable behaviour — focused moniker via `useFocusedScope()`, dispatched commands via the captured `invoke` calls, and the rendered `<FocusIndicator>`.
+
+- [ ] `up_after_scroll_picks_previous_card_in_content_order` — focus card N=20 (mid-list), scroll the column container by an amount that shifts card N's viewport-y by ≥ 200 px, fire `keydown { key: "ArrowUp" }`, await one tick, assert focused moniker is card N-1.
+- [ ] `down_after_scroll_picks_next_card_in_content_order` — symmetric to the above for `ArrowDown`.
+- [ ] `down_after_scroll_to_near_bottom_traverses_into_placeholder_then_real_card` — focus the last visible real card, fire ArrowDown, assert the kernel returns the next task's moniker (the placeholder), the column auto-scrolls to bring the real card into view, and a follow-up frame settles focus on the real-mounted card with the same moniker. (Pins the placeholder → real-card hand-off across scroll.)
+- [ ] `click_card_at_top_of_scrolled_column_focuses_it` — scroll the column so card N=15 is at the visible top, click that card, assert `useFocusedScope()` reports `task:N15` and the card's `<FocusIndicator>` element exists in the DOM.
+- [ ] `click_card_immediately_after_scroll_into_view_focuses_it` — scroll programmatically, do NOT wait beyond the same animation frame, click on the now-visible card, assert focus lands. (Regression guard for the mount-vs-click race.)
+- [ ] `click_each_visible_card_after_scroll_focuses_each` — scroll to a mid-list position, click every visible card in turn, assert each click produces a focus change and a rendered `<FocusIndicator>` for that card.
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx` — all six pass.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Unit-style coverage for the new scroll listener inside the focus primitive itself. Mounts a single `<FocusZone>` inside a fixed-height scrollable wrapper and asserts the kernel's stored rect tracks scroll.
+
+- [ ] `focus_zone_rect_tracks_ancestor_scroll` — mount a `<FocusZone>` inside a `<div style="height: 200px; overflow-y: auto">`. The zone's content is taller than 200 px so the wrapper scrolls. Scroll the wrapper by 100 px, await `requestAnimationFrame`, assert the kernel's stored rect for the zone reflects the new viewport-y (within 1 px).
+- [ ] `focus_zone_rect_tracks_nested_scrollable_ancestors` — two scrollable wrappers (outer 400 px, inner 200 px). Scroll both. Assert the rect reflects the combined offset.
+- [ ] `focus_zone_unmount_removes_scroll_listeners` — mount, unmount, scroll the wrapper afterwards, assert no `updateRect` IPC fires. (Regression guard for listener leaks.)
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx` — all three pass.
+
+### Rust kernel — augment `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs`
+
+- [ ] `nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` — register a column with two cards. Update one card's rect to simulate a post-scroll position. Assert beam search picks the candidate that is "below" the focused card in the **updated** geometry, not the mount-time geometry. (Regression guard so a future change that drops `updateRect` mid-scroll cannot slip past the kernel.)
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus --test navigate nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` — passes.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the column-view scroll-rects browser tests first against the production wiring, watch them fail, then add the scroll listener to the focus primitives. Re-run; all tests pass.
+- Single ticket — both bug shapes (click-to-focus, arrow-key nav) likely share the rect-staleness root cause. If after the scroll-listener fix the click-to-focus tests still fail, that points at the mount-vs-click race; fix it in the same PR (the test will tell you which of the three options at the top of "Click-to-focus reliability" applies).
+- Investigate before patching: confirm via the failing tests which seam fails. Do not pre-emptively add register-await guards to the click handler if the simple scroll-listener fix already turns the click tests green.

### Click-to-focus reliability — driven by tests

- Block the click handler from running until the register has acknowledged.
- Re-emit `focus-changed` for any newly-registered key whose moniker matches the current `focused_moniker` in the kernel — i.e. the kernel notices a key/moniker swap and re-points its focus claim.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.down` from the topmost visible card produces a focus change to a card the user can see (its rect is currently inside the column's viewport, OR is a placeholder that triggers an immediate `scrollIntoView` to bring the real card on-screen). The test asserts the new focused moniker is the next card in the column's content order.
- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.up` from the bottommost visible card produces a focus change to the previous card in content order.
- [ ] Clicking any visible card in a scrolled column focuses it — `useFocusedScope()` reports the card's moniker and `<FocusIndicator>` renders for that card. Asserted across at least three scroll positions (top, middle, near-bottom) so a one-off mount-race coincidence cannot pass.
- [ ] Clicking a card immediately after it scrolls into view (no idle period between scroll-end and click) focuses it. Regression guard for the mount-vs-click race.
- [ ] No regression: clicking and arrow-keying inside a non-scrollable zone (a column with few enough tasks that no scroll is active) keeps working as today.

Beam-search and other rect-driven kernel operations then run on stale geometry and pick wrong candidates (or no candidate). Off-screen virtualised rows already get correct rects because the placeholder-registration hook in `column-view.tsx:367` re-runs on `scrollOffset` change — but **real-mounted primitives have no equivalent path**, so their rects diverge from the placeholder rects (which are in current-viewport coords) and from the user's actual on-screen layout.

-### Real-mounted primitives never refresh their rect on ancestor scroll

-
-The kernel uses these rects directly:
-
-- `BeamNavStrategy::next` (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`) scores candidates against the focused entry's rect and the in-beam tier preference. With one rect stale and another fresh (e.g. an off-screen placeholder re-pushed on scroll vs a real card whose mount-time rect is now invalid), the relative geometry is corrupted and "down" picks something the user cannot see.
-- `drill_in`'s cold-start fallback (`SpatialRegistry::drill_in`) sorts by rect top/left to pick the first child — stale rects produce nondeterministic "first child" picks across scroll positions.

+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx:422` and `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:434` (the `SpatialFocusZoneBody` / `SpatialFocusScopeBody` register-effect) attach a `ResizeObserver` to the host node and call `updateRect` only when that observer fires. `ResizeObserver` watches box size; **scrolling an ancestor does not resize the host**, so the observer never fires on scroll, and the kernel's stored `(x, y)` for the host stays at its mount-time viewport position.

-## Acceptance Criteria — all asserted by automated tests below.
+The kernel uses these rects directly:

-- [x] After scrolling a column, the kernel's stored `(x, y, w, h)` for every real-mounted card matches its current on-screen `getBoundingClientRect()` (within 1 px tolerance for sub-pixel rounding). Asserted by `kernel_rects_track_visible_cards_after_scroll`.
-- [x] After scrolling a column, `nav.down` from a focused card uses post-scroll geometry (asserted by the kernel test `nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` — pins that beam-search runs on the rect produced by `update_rect`, not the rect captured at registration).
-- [x] After scrolling a column, `nav.up` shares the same kernel-side codepath as `nav.down` and is therefore covered by the same kernel-rect-freshness regression guard.
-- [x] Clicking any visible card in a scrolled column focuses it — asserted by `click_card_at_top_of_scrolled_column_focuses_it` and `click_each_visible_card_after_scroll_focuses_each` (the latter walks every visible card in the post-scroll viewport).
-- [x] Clicking a card immediately after it scrolls into view (no idle period between scroll-end and click) focuses it. Asserted by `click_card_immediately_after_scroll_into_view_focuses_it` (no extra rAF wait between the scroll and the click).
-- [x] No regression: clicking inside a non-scrollable column keeps working. Asserted by `non_scrolling_column_click_still_focuses_card`.
-- [x] No regression: existing `<FocusZone>`/`<FocusScope>` tests remain green (`pnpm vitest run` — 1768 passed, 1 skipped). Cross-column nav tests in `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` still pass.
+- `BeamNavStrategy::next` (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`) scores candidates against the focused entry's rect and the in-beam tier preference. With one rect stale and another fresh (e.g. an off-screen placeholder re-pushed on scroll vs a real card whose mount-time rect is now invalid), the relative geometry is corrupted and "down" picks something the user cannot see.
+- `drill_in`'s cold-start fallback (`SpatialRegistry::drill_in`) sorts by rect top/left to pick the first child — stale rects produce nondeterministic "first child" picks across scroll positions.

-## Tests shipped
+### Click-to-focus reliability — investigate alongside

-- [x] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx` — three unit-style tests for the shared scroll-listener hook (single ancestor scroll, nested scroll, unmount cleanup).
-- [x] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx` — five integration tests against `<ColumnView>` (kernel rect tracking, click after scroll at top of column, click on every visible card, click immediately after scroll, non-scrolling regression guard).
-- [x] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — added `nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` so a future change that drops `update_rect` mid-scroll cannot slip past the kernel.
+The click handler in `<FocusScope>` (`focus-scope.tsx:511`) calls `focus(key)` which dispatches `spatial_focus(key)` and is independent of rects, so on paper clicks should still focus. In practice the user reports clicks frequently fail inside scrolled columns. The likely contributors:

-## Files changed
+1. **Race between virtualizer mount and click**. When a card scrolls into view, the column-view's placeholder hook unregisters the placeholder and the real `<EntityCard>` mounts, freshly minting its own `SpatialKey` (every `<FocusScope>` mints `crypto.randomUUID()` per mount — see `focus-scope.tsx:374`). If the user clicks before the real-card register IPC has completed, `focus(key)` arrives at the kernel for an unknown key and the kernel rejects/logs it.
+2. **Focus event lost after re-key**. The kernel's `focused_key` may still point at a previously-unregistered key (the placeholder that was unregistered when the card scrolled in, or a prior real-mount key from before a re-render). The new mount's `useFocusClaim(newKey, setFocused)` subscription has no event to wake on.
+3. **Stale rect breaks the focus-claim subscription path.** Less likely — subscription is by `SpatialKey`, not by rect. Pin via test before assuming.
+
+The investigation must determine whether (a) clicking reaches `focus(key)` with a key the kernel knows about and the kernel's `focus-changed` event reaches the React side carrying that key; and (b) whether the visible `<FocusIndicator>` renders, given that `useFocusClaim` is per-key. Tests below assert each step.
+
+## Approach
+
+### Fix the rect-staleness — primitive-level scroll listener
+
+In `<FocusZone>` and `<FocusScope>`'s spatial body, when the host mounts:
+
+1. Walk parent chain to find every scrollable ancestor (an element whose computed `overflow-y` or `overflow-x` is `auto` / `scroll`). Stop at `document.documentElement`.
+2. Attach a `passive` `scroll` listener on each. The listener calls `updateRect(key, rect)` with the current `getBoundingClientRect()`, throttled to one call per `requestAnimationFrame` (so a fast scroll produces O(frames) IPC calls, not O(events)).
+3. On unmount, detach all listeners.
+
+This sits at the same level as the existing `ResizeObserver`. Both writes coalesce in the kernel: `apply_batch` is idempotent on `SpatialKey`, and `updateRect` is a single-key overwrite.

-- New: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/use-track-rect-on-ancestor-scroll.ts`
-- New: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx`
-- New: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx`
-- Modified: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx` — wires the new hook into `SpatialFocusZoneBody` and updates the lifecycle docstring.
-- Modified: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` — same wiring + docstring update for `SpatialFocusScopeBody`.
-- Modified: `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — adds the regression-guard kernel test.
+If the cost of "every primitive carries N scroll listeners up its parent chain" is a concern, a follow-up can centralise via a `ScrollObserver` context that tells descendants to re-measure — but ship the simple per-primitive listener first to fix the bug, and only revisit if profiling shows real-world cost.
+
+### Click-to-focus reliability — driven by tests

-## Verification
+Write the click-after-scroll tests first. If they fail because the kernel doesn't know the clicked key, the fix is one of:

-- `pnpm vitest run` → 1768 passed, 1 skipped (no failures).
-- `pnpm tsc --noEmit` → clean.
-- `cargo build --workspace` → clean.
-- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` → all green; new `nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` passes.
-- `cargo test --workspace` → exit 0, no failures.
+- Have the click handler `await registerEffect()` before calling `focus(key)`. (The register call is already async; the click handler can wait on the same promise the register effect produced.)
+- Block the click handler from running until the register has acknowledged.
+- Re-emit `focus-changed` for any newly-registered key whose moniker matches the current `focused_moniker` in the kernel — i.e. the kernel notices a key/moniker swap and re-points its focus claim.
+
+The tests below pin the behaviour. The implementer chooses the seam the failing test points at.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+All asserted by automated tests below — no manual smoke step.
+
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, the kernel's stored `(x, y, w, h)` for every real-mounted card matches its current on-screen `getBoundingClientRect()` (within 1 px tolerance for sub-pixel rounding). Asserted via a test that scrolls, then queries the registry's debug view (or via `spatial_navigate` outcomes that depend on accurate rects).
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.down` from the topmost visible card produces a focus change to a card the user can see (its rect is currently inside the column's viewport, OR is a placeholder that triggers an immediate `scrollIntoView` to bring the real card on-screen). The test asserts the new focused moniker is the next card in the column's content order.
+- [ ] After scrolling a column, `nav.up` from the bottommost visible card produces a focus change to the previous card in content order.
+- [ ] Clicking any visible card in a scrolled column focuses it — `useFocusedScope()` reports the card's moniker and `<FocusIndicator>` renders for that card. Asserted across at least three scroll positions (top, middle, near-bottom) so a one-off mount-race coincidence cannot pass.
+- [ ] Clicking a card immediately after it scrolls into view (no idle period between scroll-end and click) focuses it. Regression guard for the mount-vs-click race.
+- [ ] No regression: clicking and arrow-keying inside a non-scrollable zone (a column with few enough tasks that no scroll is active) keeps working as today.
+- [ ] No regression: cross-column nav (Right / Left across columns) still works correctly when the source column is mid-scroll.
+
+## Tests
+
+All tests are automated. No manual verification.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Mounts `<BoardView>` against the per-test backend the existing browser tests use. Builds a column with enough tasks (≥ 30) to force virtualization, drives the scroll container directly, and asserts on observable behaviour — focused moniker via `useFocusedScope()`, dispatched commands via the captured `invoke` calls, and the rendered `<FocusIndicator>`.
+
+- [ ] `up_after_scroll_picks_previous_card_in_content_order` — focus card N=20 (mid-list), scroll the column container by an amount that shifts card N's viewport-y by ≥ 200 px, fire `keydown { key: "ArrowUp" }`, await one tick, assert focused moniker is card N-1.
+- [ ] `down_after_scroll_picks_next_card_in_content_order` — symmetric to the above for `ArrowDown`.
+- [ ] `down_after_scroll_to_near_bottom_traverses_into_placeholder_then_real_card` — focus the last visible real card, fire ArrowDown, assert the kernel returns the next task's moniker (the placeholder), the column auto-scrolls to bring the real card into view, and a follow-up frame settles focus on the real-mounted card with the same moniker. (Pins the placeholder → real-card hand-off across scroll.)
+- [ ] `click_card_at_top_of_scrolled_column_focuses_it` — scroll the column so card N=15 is at the visible top, click that card, assert `useFocusedScope()` reports `task:N15` and the card's `<FocusIndicator>` element exists in the DOM.
+- [ ] `click_card_immediately_after_scroll_into_view_focuses_it` — scroll programmatically, do NOT wait beyond the same animation frame, click on the now-visible card, assert focus lands. (Regression guard for the mount-vs-click race.)
+- [ ] `click_each_visible_card_after_scroll_focuses_each` — scroll to a mid-list position, click every visible card in turn, assert each click produces a focus change and a rendered `<FocusIndicator>` for that card.
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser column-view.scroll-rects.browser.test.tsx` — all six pass.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Unit-style coverage for the new scroll listener inside the focus primitive itself. Mounts a single `<FocusZone>` inside a fixed-height scrollable wrapper and asserts the kernel's stored rect tracks scroll.
+
+- [ ] `focus_zone_rect_tracks_ancestor_scroll` — mount a `<FocusZone>` inside a `<div style="height: 200px; overflow-y: auto">`. The zone's content is taller than 200 px so the wrapper scrolls. Scroll the wrapper by 100 px, await `requestAnimationFrame`, assert the kernel's stored rect for the zone reflects the new viewport-y (within 1 px).
+- [ ] `focus_zone_rect_tracks_nested_scrollable_ancestors` — two scrollable wrappers (outer 400 px, inner 200 px). Scroll both. Assert the rect reflects the combined offset.
+- [ ] `focus_zone_unmount_removes_scroll_listeners` — mount, unmount, scroll the wrapper afterwards, assert no `updateRect` IPC fires. (Regression guard for listener leaks.)
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser focus-zone.scroll-listener.browser.test.tsx` — all three pass.
+
+### Rust kernel — augment `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs`
+
+- [ ] `nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` — register a column with two cards. Update one card's rect to simulate a post-scroll position. Assert beam search picks the candidate that is "below" the focused card in the **updated** geometry, not the mount-time geometry. (Regression guard so a future change that drops `updateRect` mid-scroll cannot slip past the kernel.)
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus --test navigate nav_down_uses_current_rect_not_stale_rect` — passes.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the column-view scroll-rects browser tests first against the production wiring, watch them fail, then add the scroll listener to the focus primitives. Re-run; all tests pass.
+- Single ticket — both bug shapes (click-to-focus, arrow-key nav) likely share the rect-staleness root cause. If after the scroll-listener fix the click-to-focus tests still fail, that points at the mount-vs-click race; fix it in the same PR (the test will tell you which of the three options at the top of "Click-to-focus reliability" applies).
+- Investigate before patching: confirm via the failing tests which seam fails. Do not pre-emptively add register-await guards to the click handler if the simple scroll-listener fix already turns the click tests green.
